name: beta(G,W) == beta(G-e,W) for non-bridge non-wrap e (any G)
hypothesis: e not a bridge of G, e not in W

name: Adding a pendant non-wrap edge preserves beta
hypothesis: new vertex w, new edge (v, w) not in W

name: Adding a pendant wrap edge preserves beta
hypothesis: new vertex w, new edge (v, w) in W

name: Non-wrap edge swap (remove e, add e') preserves beta when e, e' both non-wrap non-bridge and connectivity preserved
hypothesis: G connected; e in E\W non-bridge; e' not in E; connected after swap; e' not in W; e' not a bridge of result

name: Contracting a non-bridge non-wrap edge preserves beta
hypothesis: e non-bridge, e not in W, G connected

name: Contract wrap edge e after switching at one endpoint preserves beta
hypothesis: e in W; W' = switch_v(W) for v an endpoint of e; then e becomes non-wrap, contract

name: Iterated k-fold subdivision of non-wrap edge preserves beta for k in {2,3}
hypothesis: e not in W; k fresh vertices inserted, all new edges non-wrap

name: Subdividing edge e (wrap or non-wrap) into k parts s.t. new-wrap-count has same parity as [e in W] preserves beta
hypothesis: k in {2,3}; new wrap parity matches old wrap parity on e

name: Adding 2 new non-wrap edges to G preserves beta
hypothesis: e1, e2 not in E(G); both non-wrap; e1 != e2

name: Order of adding 2 edges is irrelevant for beta
hypothesis: e1, e2 not in E(G), any wrap assignment; add in either order

name: W bipartite (as a graph on V) implies beta(G,W) == beta(G, E\W)
Theta-replacement of a NON-wrap edge by two non-wrap length-2 paths preserves beta
hypothesis: e not in W; replace by 2 parallel length-2 paths via fresh w1, w2, all new edges non-wrap

name: beta(G, W) == beta(G, switch_v W) for any vertex v
Flipping wrap status of a single non-bridge edge preserves beta
